choices: Large investors may be net selling into strength — a potential caution flag
diagnosticQuestions.hint: Think handing shares from strong hands to weaker hands — a classic framing.

choices: Buying interest may be building during a constructive phase
diagnosticQuestions.hint: Absorbing supply — a possible foundation for uptrends (not a guarantee).

teachSlides.title: The big picture filter
teachSlides.body: Many investors check whether the broad market is in an uptrend, under pressure, or in a correction. Your stock’s setup can fail if the tide turns.

teachSlides.title: Follow-through (concept)
teachSlides.body: Some methods look for a strong follow-through day after a bottoming attempt to suggest risk-on may be justified. Rules vary — learn one system fully.

teachSlides.title: Not timing advice
teachSlides.body: These are lenses for risk control, not crystal balls. Combine with your own rules and position size.

recapSlide.title: Recap
recapSlide.body: Accumulation vs distribution are ways to talk about whether large participation is net building or net selling — use as context, not prophecy.

masteryQuestions.prompt: Why care about overall market health?
choices: Individual stocks can be dragged by the tide
masteryQuestions.explanation: Broad pressure can hurt even good setups.

masteryQuestions.prompt: A follow-through day in the common O’Neil-style story is used to:
choices: Help signal that a rally attempt may be gaining credibility (per that method)
masteryQuestions.explanation: It’s one checkpoint in a larger market-direction toolkit.

masteryQuestions.prompt: Distribution days are often interpreted as:
choices: Possible signs of selling into strength — worth noting as a caution input
masteryQuestions.explanation: They count selling pressure in context — not a single switch.
